heading: Authorization of appropriations for payment of United States expenses in Organization; limitation of contributions
label: 279a Authorization of appropriations for payment of United States expenses in Organization; limitation of contributions
text: There is hereby authorized to be appropriated, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, such sums as may be required for expenditure under the direction of the Secretary of State, for the payment by the United States of its proportionate share in the expenses of the Organization: , That the percentage contribution of the United States to the total annual budget of the Organization shall not exceed 33.33 per centum.Provided
notes: Amendments1961— struck out provisions which limited the annual appropriations to not more than $3,000,000.Pub. L. 87–1951957—, added section 544(b) to act , which section 544(b) amended this section by increasing the maximum percentage contribution from 31.5 to 33.33 per centum.Pub. L. 85–141, § 11(b)(2)August 26, 19541956—Act , increased authorized annual appropriation from $2,000,000, to $3,000,000, and inserted provisions limiting percentage contribution of United States to total annual budget to not more than 31.5 per centum.July 18, 19561950—Joint Res. , increased authorized annual appropriation from $1,250,000 to $2,000,000.Sept. 21, 1950

Annual AppropriationsAnnual appropriations to meet the obligations of membership in various international organizations were contained in acts listed in a note set out under .section 269a of this title

Limitation of ContributionsContributions by United States, except for special projects, limited to amount provided by Joint Res. , consent by State Department and reports to Congress, see .Sept. 21, 1950section 262a of this title
